why_winner: The parallel developer portraits make the core insight structurally visible from line one — readers immediately recognise themselves in one of the two portraits. The specific detail ('The AI keeps producing the same bugs. They keep copy-pasting, hoping something sticks.') feels lived-in rather than constructed. The split-screen structure also aligns with the strongest performance signal in the tracker (comparison framing drove the account's top engagement rate).

runner_up_notes.B: The opening inversion ('The developer who ignores AI is not the one falling behind') is sharper than C's first line — not borrowed this round since C's parallel structure is the stronger overall frame, but worth noting for future bold-claim-first posts.
runner_up_notes.A: The rhetorical questions in A add warmth and feel conversational; the pattern of answering real questions from developers grounds the advice in lived experience.
